mnemonics: SIGNS Point To Words: Signature files use a Signature (a hash) to Point to documents containing a certain Word.
keyPoints: Signature files are a filtering technique used to quickly eliminate non-relevant documents before a more costly detailed search.
A signature is a hash (a bitmask) created for each word in a document.
Documents that pass the signature filter are then checked with a more precise method, like scanning for the exact query terms.
commonMistakes: Assuming a positive match from a signature file guarantees the document contains the word; it only indicates it might (false positives are possible).
Confusing signature files with inverted indexes; signatures are a less precise, faster pre-filter, while inverted indexes directly map words to documents.
